privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task involving a neighborhood tool library damage scenario. The core facts verified: A Pressure Washer was damaged (nozzle) with repair cost of $85. The damage period is May 15-20, 2024. During this period, two borrowers had possession: Jessica Lee (May 14-18, overlapping 4 days of damage period) and Tom Chen (May 19-22, overlapping 2 days). Based on proportional allocation, Jessica Lee should bear $56.67 of repair costs (4 days) and Tom Chen should bear $28.33 (2 days). The scenario includes intentionally messy data with mixed date formats (5/3/24, May 5 2024, 2024-05-14, 5-10-2024, etc.), missing return dates (Mike Roberts), and conflicting information to create a realistic data cleaning challenge. The task requires standardizing dates, inferring missing data (Mike's return date = May 13), identifying damage-period borrowers, calculating proportional costs, and applying conditional formatting. Verifier uses 70% threshold (4 of 6 criteria) for success.
